content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of three lines. The first line contains two integers n and d, where n is the number of elements in the array and d is the value added to each element with a certain probability. The second line contains n integers representing the array B. The third line contains n integers representing the probabilities P.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the expected number of swaps that bubble sorting will make when the array A is sorted with the above bubble sorting algorithm. The output should be rounded to exactly 4 digits after the decimal point.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the expected number of swaps is 0.25 because the probability of swapping the two elements is 0.5 and the probability of not swapping is 0.5. For the second test case, the expected number of swaps is 1.6049 because the probabilities of swapping the elements are 0.25, 0.25, and 0.5 respectively.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by iterating over all pairs of elements in the array and calculating the probability that they will be swapped during the bubble sort. This probability depends on the values of the elements and their corresponding probabilities. The expected number of swaps can then be calculated by summing up these probabilities for all pairs of elements.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n^2) per test case, O(T * n^2) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) — for storing the arrays B and P.
